coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, 14 weeks to the Julian Alps 50K. Friday is your hill-strength day: 75–90 min on the steepest sustained climb you can find, holding Zone 2–low Zone 3 on the ups and walking the punchiest grades with poles. Power-hike anything over ~15%, exactly as you will on the 2,500m of alpine vert in Slovenia. If you're traveling and stuck flat, sub a 45-min treadmill incline session at 8–12% or a stadium/parking-garage stair repeat block. Focus cue: quiet feet, tall posture, drive from the glutes — not the calves.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Train the gut now so race day is boring. On today's long climb, take in 60–80g carbs/hour starting at minute 30 — alternate a gel (~22g) every 25 min with a few sips of a 6% carb drink. Practice the exact products you'll race with in Kranjska Gora. Afterward, hit 25–35g protein plus ~1g carb/kg bodyweight within 45 min (e.g., Greek yogurt with granola and a banana) to bank the adaptation while you're moving between time zones.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: After a hill day, the calves and Achilles take the hit. Spend 8–10 min tonight foam-rolling calves, quads, and glutes, then 2 min each side in a wall-supported calf/soleus stretch. Aim for 7.5–8h sleep, and if you're flying, wear compression socks on the leg and hydrate to bodyweight-adjusted needs — dehydration from cabin air blunts the recovery you just earned.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Build Phase is where the race is quietly won — not in the taper, not on race day, but in showing up for the unglamorous climbs while juggling MNTN and a passport full of stamps. Every steep rep today is a deposit you'll withdraw at the top of an Alpine pass in September. When the legs protest, shrink the task: just get to the next switchback. Mountains reward patience, and so does this block.
